Drums of the Beast  
 

Lore Item Placeable
Slot: PRIMARY SECONDARY
Charges: Unlimited
This item is placeable in yards, guild yards, houses and guild halls.
Effect: Enduring Breath (Any Slot/Can Equip, Casting Time: Instant)
Focus: Percussion Resonance 16
WT: 1.0 Size: MEDIUM
Class: BRD
Race: ALL
Slot 1, Type 7 (General: Group)
Drums of the Beast
Lore Item Placeable
Slot: PRIMARY SECONDARY
Charges: Unlimited
This item is placeable in yards, guild yards, houses and guild halls.
Effect: Enduring Breath (Any Slot/Can Equip, Casting Time: Instant)
Focus: Percussion Resonance 16
WT: 1.0 Size: MEDIUM
Class: BRD
Race: ALL
Slot 1, Type 7 (General: Group)

Item Lore:Drums of the Sea Beasts
Item Type:Percussion Instrument
Stackable:No
Tribute:77
Submitted By:Jadae Pacifica
Lucy Entry By:Kerasota
Item Updated By:SwiftyMUSE
Source:Live
IC Last Updated:2022-01-02 13:09:51
Page Updated:Sat Oct 28th, 2017

Expansion: Scars of Velious
Average Price: 75,000pp Pricing Data...
Rarity: Rare
Level to Attain: 50
[Drops | Comments ]

Drops

This item is found on creatures.

Siren's Grotto
NPC Name
shimmering sea spirit


Zone(s) Found In:


Zone Name
Siren's Grotto
Formerly dropped by Mistress Latazura, confirmed to no longer drop by GM as of November 21, 2013. Reinstated around 2015. Now drops off shimmering sea spirit.

Drums of the Beast item window 2016

Uploaded October 30th, 2016 by snailish
Send a correction
« Previous 1 2 Next »
Post Comment
Available once again on all servers.
QuoteReply
# May 31 2016 at 1:00 PM Rating: Excellent
Nniki
Guru
297 posts
This is once again available on all servers as a drop from the Shimmering Sea Spirit.

Placeholders are a watery servant and an icy servant. There are 20 spawn points.
▲ Page top
GM Confirmation - Drums of the Beast No Longer Drops
QuoteReply
# Nov 27 2013 at 5:04 PM Rating: Decent
Ringkeeper
Scholar
45 posts
Quote:
Subject
Mistress Latazura - Drums of the Beast?

Discussion Thread
Response Via Email (Game Master) 11/21/2013 02:31 PM
Hail N---,

Thank you for contacting Sony Online Entertainment Customer Service. GM ----- here!

After reviewing our logs, it looks like the Drums of the Beast do not drop off of Mistress Latazura. Please keep in mind that all items are subject to change at any time. I hope this helps and I wish you the best of luck!

For your convenience, I will leave this ticket open for the next 72 hours. If you have any other questions or concerns pertaining to this situation please feel free to update this petition within the given time frame and I will be more than happy to help out!

Good Day,
GM -----
Sony Online Entertainment

Knowledge Base: http://help.soe.com

▲ Page top
Camp's difficulty
QuoteReply
# Jul 06 2003 at 12:28 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592257952954
1 post
I was lucky enough to see these drums drop on my last visit to Siren's Grotto. A 61 bard and I, a 55 cleric, were able to take down any placeholders and the named. It definitely wasn't easy, but with the bard tanking and twisting mana regen, slow, and melee buffs we were pretty safe through what proved to be long, drawn out fights.

Most mobs were very magic resistsant - at 55 I landed at about 40% success. The placeholders do ENRAGE, cannot be rooted, snared, or charmed, but can be mezzed.

Also, we were able to take out Fellspine (TOUGH fight in which we would have died if not for the fact that I had KEI). .. Oh, and we got the haste belt ;)

Embries
55 Dorf Cleric - Vazaelle
http://www.magelo.com/eq_view_profile.html?num=701942
▲ Page top
Drms
QuoteReply
# Jun 30 2003 at 4:13 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592258182539
6 posts
These Drums are still one of the best in game (and definately the best drums for twinks), had my bard using a walrus skin drum at lvl 46, this hit for 55hp with tuyans chant of flame, with drums of the beast at lvl 46 i hit for 62, which when you stack 3 Dots is ALOT of extra dps.



Edited, Mon Jun 30 04:39:43 2003

oh yeah
QuoteReply
# May 07 2003 at 6:11 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591788271798
38 posts
the post about dire charming there. i went with 63 enchie and there were virually no dire charmable mobs. she tried on the lowest level mobs there(enthralled molkors, bulthars, watery servants) with maxxed cha and it being as MR debuffed as possible(no idea if that would help but after 4 hours of camping drum and 3 failed DC attempts we decided to try it). NO DICE guess they nerfed that like there like i did many other places so enchies don't become the best solo class in game after their direcharm level.

QuoteReply
# May 22 2003 at 3:44 PM Rating: Excellent
__DEL__1592252007837
Scholar
104 posts
the mobs i dire charm are the walrusses , manatees an sea elephants , the are still DC able but only when they are of the lowest level they can possibly be, you may have to clear a few before you get one that is of a low enough level.
▲ Page top
bard solo
QuoteReply
# May 07 2003 at 6:06 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591788271798
38 posts
a level 62 bard has NO shot to solo him that i can think of. i am 55 now and went there to get my drum(still no luck after 2 weeks or trying got me a neriad shawl and lyndellns lute tho) The icy placeholders are MEAN they got nerfed about year or 2 ago but still mean. They have a dmg shield thats in the 50+pts per shot range, fair resits, TONS(didn't calculate but i am guessing 10-20k hp range) of hp. they never spawn alone on land, tho there are a few areas where you could pull single ones.

MIGHT, repeat MIGHT have a PRAYER to solo one with charms but i really don't see it. His dmg shield alone took our warlord(61 5500hp) down to about 60 life before enhie coudl disspell it and it was the first spell she cast upon pull.

No, if you can solo one it would be a miracle. might be possible but i dont see how. find a way, post it on here.
▲ Page top
Solo???
QuoteReply
# Apr 10 2003 at 1:32 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591755751308
95 posts
Could a level 62 druid solo the shimmering Sea Spirit with no trouble getting to it? and how often do these drums drop?
▲ Page top
RE: Solo???
QuoteReply
# May 05 2003 at 11:32 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592252007837
Scholar
104 posts
This really depends upon where it spawns at any given time ,the shimmering sea servant can spawn anywhere a icy/watery servant spawns on land.
some locations are far easier to get to than others, None of them are right near a zone in so you will most likely need to kill a few mobs to get to them.

If you have dire charm and the shimmering is in a good spot its fairly easy to solo. if you dont have dire charm or its in a bad spot and you dont know the zone you will likely die trying.

The reason I mention dire charm is the fact that the shimmering , its placeholder and almost all of the mobs that spawn underwater are unrootable/unsnareable and often come in groups. The pet can act as a tank. Dire charmed Pets here make excellent tanks due to the large number of hp they have. the lvl 58 large druid heal only heals about 1 bubble on the servant sea elephants.

Overall I would not suggest trying to do this solo if you have never hunted in SG, play around near the zone ins for a while and learn the zones quirks and pathing, this zone can be very unforgiving if you mess up.

Edited, Mon May 5 11:58:20 2003
▲ Page top

QuoteReply
# Jan 02 2003 at 7:47 PM Rating: Decent

RE: 30k??
QuoteReply
# May 14 2003 at 7:50 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592256292256
244 posts
30k is fairly low. I see them go for 40k all the time. There is only one other tradeable drum in game that is better than it and only has a slight increase to the percussion mod. It goes for 70k on Karana. It has alot of stats but other than HP/STA or maybe INT/CHA if charming what the hell good is stats on a drum anyway lol.
▲ Page top
#REDACTED, Posted: Nov 13 2002 at 9:37 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) Wanting to buy one of these.. contact tommythree on MT. server paying well =0
Clarification
QuoteReply
# Aug 24 2002 at 12:39 PM Rating: Good
__DEL__1591781414413
6 posts
To Clarify: I am not sure as to the past, but as of now these drums greatly increase the effect of selos at a run. Considering with epic off hand, there is a very noticable difference in speed when I drop these drums onto main hand.

Also, On Elemental chorus, there is a 30 to 40 point difference when I equip this compared to straight epic.

These are hands down the best drums in game.

One other factor that many people are missing about these drums is this. The effect on the drums is right click targetable. This makes on hell of a dummy buff when fighting any dispelling mob.

Considering you stack this with lute of howler, and illusion mask, you are giving yourself 3 dummies against any dispelling mobs.

Edited, Sat Aug 24 13:29:25 2002
▲ Page top
RE: Clarification
QuoteReply
# Sep 20 2002 at 8:52 AM Rating: Excellent
__DEL__1592759747675
10 posts
They were the best drum in the game, now theres a drum in Vex Thal that drops, Denons drums of declivityDenon's Drums of Declivity
LORE ITEM MAGIC ITEM NO DROP
AC: +20 Str: +12 Sta: +12 Dex: +12 Cha: +12 Poison Resist: +25 Disease Resist: +25 HP: +125 Mana: +125
Weight: 2.0
Weapon Skill: Bardsong
Percent to Bardsong: 0.00
Classes: Bard
Races: All Races
Inventory Slot: Either Melee
Can be equipped in the range slot
Can be stored in Small and larger containers
Last updated: Thu Sep 12 16:08:54 2002

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Effect: Occlusion of Sound
Type: Triggered
Charges: Unlimited

▲ Page top
RE: Clarification
QuoteReply
# Mar 17 2003 at 7:27 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592756642848
4 posts
While I wouldn't let DDoD rot with such amazing stats on the rare occasion that they do drop in Vex Thal, they certainly would not replace my drums of the beast as my primary percussion instrument.

I'd keep them in range for the stats but when I look for best drums in the game, personally I mean the actual mod to bard songs, In other words, song effect is everything, stats on an instrument mean nothing.

Also, occlusion of sound would be better memorized and played with a better drum (for egsample; drums of the beast) than this clickable one because instruments do not have any effect on clickable item songs. And as you can see from your post about the Vex Thal drum, the percent modifier to bard songs is 0.0%, so no increase. Its all about ranged stats.

So for clarification, 0.0 percent is not better than 44.44 percent.... well maybe on your next credit card statement.... or if you're a monk.

Edited, Mon Mar 17 18:54:15 2003
▲ Page top
RE: Clarification
QuoteReply
# Nov 12 2002 at 5:38 PM Rating: Good
__DEL__1592254675818
160 posts
These no longer have a better mod than puretone. =( DotB are better again. =\
▲ Page top
OOS
QuoteReply
# Aug 20 2002 at 5:30 AM Rating: Good
__DEL__1591770377803
73 posts
This drum lets you melee and easily switch out your primary weapon for the drum for a second while casting OOS and other resist songs, then replacing your primary weapon back in its spot. If you dont have this drum you have to either not melee if you are primarily doing resist type songs or switch out both weapons to put in a drum. Thats why this drum is so bad ***. Similar to lute of the howler and a couple other lutes out there that you can wield a weapon with, when i melee and am playing cantana of replenishment i can replace my primary with a lute just before the song is about to cast and replace it in about a second to get good effects from the lute while i continue to melee. I could give a rats *** about that EB thats not what makes this drum so desirable.
▲ Page top
LOL
QuoteReply
# Jul 24 2002 at 6:53 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591780648480
Scholar
60 posts
Hehe just got it 2 days ago and I really LOVE it!!!
The funny part of this drum: Go in EC with the drum, make a hotkey with it, target yourself, run selo (49) and dont stop hitting hotkey while running.....LOL => You will look like Dragon ball Z :-), and the all zone is going to shout or ooc : OMG!!!! hehe

And for the question just below : yes it look like a tambourine ;)
This is the must for a bard!

# Jul 01 2002 at 1:02 AM Rating: Default

3 posts
When i equip this, it looks like a tambourine. Is this right?

▲ Page top
Just to clarify
QuoteReply
# Jun 06 2002 at 6:32 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592250375808
63 posts
Just to clarify a few things. I have this drum, and it's awesome, the fixed the patch about it not letting us go zoom zoom with it on without having to walk, and, needless to say, we do fly. Yes, it can be used with a weapon in the offhand. And I wish to get one of the special secondary instruments to test this, however, I simply do not have one. And, finally, lets just say my dots have never caused more damage. Simply put, I don't even care if I have weapons anymore, my three dot combo is just plain wrong. Granted, I totally need weapons for fear kiting, but, I only ever fear kite against something that summons that I have to kill, such as Nezekezena, Eldrig, those kinds of fun mobs. And, since swarm kiting is the simple most effective form of kiting for us bards, the drum is kinda pointless there, but, it's no bother, because I just love taking down a jerk off like Gorgul Paclock in less time solo then it takes most groups. Hehe, hello instant 2k, drums pay for themselves, effort, or money, no doubt there. As a bard, I doubt you truely need any armor at all, unless you plan on doing raids, because solo, you can go with nothing more then your drum, and if you don't believe me, then you've obviously never played a bard. And, if you want proof of that, then make a newby character on Vazz, and I'll go naked kite every gaurd in WFP with nothing but my drum.

Edited, Thu Jun 6 07:24:05 2002

QuoteReply
# Nov 23 2001 at 8:46 AM Rating: Default

I have beast drums and the effect is instant casting enduring breath i like to cast on everyone in east commons : ) only thing u cant be singing but no biggie.

▲ Page top
RE: Some Questions Answered
QuoteReply
# Nov 07 2001 at 5:29 PM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
I am also responding to Presarc's post "Here is reason..." Presarc, you should read Psycoholic's post on Percentages (found in the posts on Alluring Horn, which is listed as having -0.99 percent to bard song). He does a good job of explaining how percentages work. When it is listed as 0% it doesn't mean it doesn't increase the song at all. Quoting Psycoholic's post:

The percentages that people give for instruments are usually using storebaught instruments as the base (so an unfinished brass trumpet from a merchant would be 0%). This doesn't mean that instruments from merchants don't effect songs, nor does it mean that this instrument makes songs less effective. It simply means that this instrument effects songs 0.99% less than a brass instrument from a merchant. It makes songs better... just not much better.

As for Celestieal, you said you run faster with no drum equipped. How so??? My bard is only 20 right now and with the Walrus Skin Drum equipped(said to increase bard songs 23%), she already runs way faster than SOW, whereas without a drum equipped she runs about the same speed as SOW.

As for the whole equipping a weapon and instrument at the same time argument, I can't say I know the answers to that one. So far all the instruments I have are equipped in Secondary Slot only and can not be equipped with another weapon (or another instrument).

-Symfoni Orkestra, 20 Bard
▲ Page top
RE: Some Questions Answered
QuoteReply
# Nov 19 2001 at 2:18 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591762844758
6 posts
From what I hear, the "Run faster /w no drum" argument is true. The usual theory is that /w drums of the beast, selo's breaks the limit on increase of movement, and so resets, due to a bug. Ducking and sneaking together is supposed to "slow" you down enough enough not to break said limit, but as I do not own the drums, I haven't had the chance to test this.

Either that, or Verant won't let Drums of the Beast work with Selo's so they don't have to add horizontal impact damage. ;)
▲ Page top
Here is reason...
QuoteReply
# Oct 30 2001 at 3:21 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592757911922
29 posts
the reason they dont increase speed songs is because there is a speed hack out there, and with this drum + selo's song of travel it would be as fast as that, and the game would recognize as a hack...

So...

:-)

Vamp
▲ Page top
Some Questions Answered
QuoteReply
# Aug 06 2001 at 12:47 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591762234967
5 posts
This Instrument is an uncommon/rare drop off a Shimmering Spirit, around the low 50's (conned even to me at level 52). This mob is rare, probably on a PH (can't tell yet), and drops other things, one of them the Wavecrasher.

The percentage to *MOST* Bard songs is 55%. Selo's line of songs actually have a negative effect (I run faster with no drum equiped).

This instrument IS Primary slot ONLY. You CAN equip a weapon (or anything else thats Secondary slot) in your Secondary slot, just like Lute of the Howler. The exeption is other Instruments that are Sendondary Slot (havn't tested the PoA Horn or VP Lute theory).

The right click effect is *TARGET* Endure Breath, with an INSTANT cast time, versus the Singing Steel Gauntlets, which is a Perma-Effect.


We had about 4 groups, level range 46 - 60 in the area where this mob spawn. Could easily do 2 groups level 55 - 60 with good resist gear.


Celestieal Tunemaker
Minstrel of the 52nd Song
<Travelers of Norrath>
▲ Page top
That's incredible
QuoteReply
# Jun 18 2001 at 1:41 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592248518565
113 posts
Do people sell these? Who drops these? Is the camp easy? Can I solo these (enchanter 52)?
▲ Page top
The real story on these things
QuoteReply
# Apr 26 2001 at 11:38 PM Rating: Excellent
__DEL__1592245810567
2 posts
-Seems to add about 45% to percussion songs (except running songs-currently slows you to normal run speed)
-Effect is right-clickable and targetable
-Is primary hand only and DOES allow use of a one-handed type thing in your off-hand (other instruments are two-handed)
▲ Page top
RE: The real story on these things
QuoteReply
# May 20 2001 at 2:05 AM Rating: Good
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
They don't "slow" you to normal run speed, they roll over your speed counter to more than 200%. The engine can't comprehend that, so it ignores the 2xx and sets it to xx. Try turning on walk when you use these with selo's and you'll fly.
▲ Page top
RE: The real story on these things
QuoteReply
# Jul 16 2001 at 6:23 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592248322158
282 posts
That's probably the most insightful thing i've ever seen in a comment here. Thank you.
▲ Page top
#REDACTED, Posted: Feb 04 2002 at 8:23 AM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) ur equipment suxx big time specially ur resist gear get some blue diamond :)
#Anonymous, Posted: Feb 21 2001 at 10:52 AM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) Its useable in Primary Hand...
RE: Primary Hand...
QuoteReply
# Feb 22 2001 at 2:52 AM Rating: Good
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
Not only that, but there are a few instruments out there that are just-Secondary-only (I.e. you can still put something in the primary). I believe the Sky quest flute works this way, as well as the lute from VP.

Unfortunately, AFAIK no bard on any server has both just-Primary and just-Secondary instruments to see if dual-instrument wielding works correctly.
▲ Page top
#Anonymous, Posted: Mar 03 2001 at 9:34 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) Hmm, you know every Bard on every server do you? FYI ALL the old instruments are secondary only. Every Bard has just secondary instruments. As soon as any Bard gets this they can test the dual wield ability. Just secondary means just that. You can not put it in your primary hand. It does not stand for dual wieldable.
#REDACTED, Posted: Mar 12 2001 at 3:05 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) Did it occur to you that if you can't equip a weapon in your first hand while using an instrument, you probably can't equip an instrument either? =P The original poster is right, you'd need single hand primary and single hand secondary instruments to test this.
RE: Hey Jackass...
QuoteReply
# Jul 21 2001 at 11:25 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592248322158
282 posts
Yeah, let's call someone offering his opinion on something a jackass. & he's talking about specifically known instruments that go in the off hand, & can be used w/ a weapon in the main hand.
▲ Page top
#Anonymous, Posted: Mar 30 2001 at 5:58 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) to the jackass poster, YES you can equip a weapon in you sec hand with this in your primary, try being a bard that has one BEFORE you post... lamer... ;)
